sn12.31:1.0.1: On one occasion the Blessed One was dwelling at Sāvatthī. … There the Blessed One addressed the Venerable Sāriputta thus: "Sāriputta, in ‘The Questions of Ajita’ of the Parayana it is said:
sn12.31:1.0.3: How should the meaning of this, stated in brief, be understood in detail?"
sn12.31:1.0.4: A second time and a third time the Blessed One addressed the Venerable Sāriputta thus: "Sāriputta, in ‘The Questions of Ajita’ in the Parayana it is said … A second time and a third time the Venerable Sāriputta was silent.sn.ii.48
sn12.31:1.0.6: How should the meaning of this, stated in brief, be understood in detail?"
sn12.31:1.0.7: "Sāriputta, do you see: ‘This has come to be’? Sāriputta, do you see: ‘This has come to be’?"
sn12.31:1.0.8: "And how, venerable sir, has one comprehended the Dhamma? Venerable sir, one sees as it really is with correct wisdom: ‘This has come to be.’ Having seen as it really is with correct wisdom: ‘This has come to be,’ through revulsion towards what has come to be, through its fading away and cessation, one is liberated by nonclinging. One sees as it really is with correct wisdom: ‘Its origination occurs with that as nutriment.’ Having seen as it really is with correct wisdom: ‘Its origination occurs with that as nutriment,’ through revulsion towards its origination through nutriment, through its fading away and cessation, one is liberated by nonclinging. One sees as it really is with correct wisdom: ‘With the cessation of that nutriment, what has come to be is subject to cessation.’ Having seen as it really is with correct wisdom: ‘With the cessation of that nutriment, what has come to be is subject to cessation,’ through revulsion towards what is subject to cessation, through its fading awaysn.ii.49and cessation, one is liberated by nonclinging.It is in such a way that one has comprehended the Dhamma.
sn12.31:1.0.11: "Good, good, Sāriputta! …the Buddha repeats here the entire statement of the Venerable Sāriputtasn.ii.50… it is in such a way that the meaning of this, stated in brief, should be understood in detail."
